loop.observation: Provider real CLI checks produced useful evidence, but product, Figma, release, and repair coverage were still inferred from separate artifacts.
loop.hypothesis: A local acceptance coverage manifest can make verified, skipped, failed, and not-verified product surfaces visible before release.
loop.execute: Render provider evidence and scenario coverage into the local QA dashboard, then publish the resulting evidence JSON, HTML, and screenshot.
loop.evaluate: The dashboard and tests fail or surface gaps when provider evidence is stale, repair classification is missing, or product/Figma/release scenarios remain unverified.
loop.retrospective: Provider QA remains a daily input while uncovered product acceptance scenarios become explicit queue items instead of hidden confidence debt.

evidence_artifacts.proves: provider evidence, dashboard rendering, run evidence, and optional S3 publication execute as one local acceptance gate
macOS launchd can schedule the local acceptance gate once per day and include product acceptance evidence when present
provider real CLI status, freshness, and repair classification are machine-readable
local acceptance coverage and repair status are rendered into a reviewable dashboard
product acceptance evidence from the real frontend updates product coverage rows without mocks

scenarios.title: Contract UI golden screens match generated SaaS contract
Development user can log in from local frontend
Runtime settings page renders real development devices response
Agent settings page renders real development bootstrap response
Agent create form can be opened with real runtime/model options
User can create/update an AI agent
Task can assign multiple provider agents without collision
Codex executes task and control-plane/UI reflect result
Onboarding flow visual contract is compared with Figma intent
Runtime settings visual contract is compared with Figma intent
Fresh macOS install verifies daemon update and provider setup
